project: AutoStudio AI — Landing Page + Dashboard VISUAL/CSS Redesign (Design-Only Pass)
framing: This is a DESIGN-ONLY pass. You are restyling existing pages, not rebuilding them. Do not rewrite components, do not change data flow, do not change routes.
hard_constraints: Do NOT touch any backend code, API routes, services, or database logic (backend/** is off-limits).

Do NOT change the structure, layout architecture, components, or data wiring of the dashboard (the 3-column dashboard layout and its components — sidebar, studio grid, photo guide, upload zones, right panel — were already redesigned separately and must stay structurally as-is).

This pass is CSS / visual design / styling ONLY: colors, typography, spacing, radius, shadows, motion, tokens, responsive behavior. No new features, no new pages, no new data sources, no renamed props/routes.
All existing routes, links, auth-gated navigation, and i18n keys must keep working exactly as they do now.
Any new or changed UI copy must be added to BOTH frontend/messages/en.json and sv.json — never hardcoded.
ground_truth_rule: Do not report anything as 'done' without pasting actual evidence: screenshots of the rendered pages at desktop AND mobile (375px) widths, a tsc --noEmit output with zero errors, and a git diff --stat proving only frontend style/markup-level files changed (no backend/**, no changes to dashboard component logic/data-fetching).
scope_pages: Landing page (marketing/home page, whatever its current route is — verify the real path first)
Dashboard shell + all dashboard sub-pages: Billing, Settings, Account (visual restyle only — do not change their structure or logic, only their look)
Global design tokens (Tailwind config + globals.css :root variables) since the color clash lives there

problems_to_fix.problem: Flat, utilitarian look — plain white cards on flat gray, no depth, no motion. Feels like an internal tool, not a premium AI product.
problems_to_fix.fix: Add depth via layered shadows (--shadow-card / --shadow-card-lg / --shadow-card-hover), soft ambient background gradient (cream → soft-lilac), and restrained motion (see Motion section). Cards should float, not sit flat.

problems_to_fix.problem: Two clashing accent colors. Top-nav active state, the logo 'AI', and Account 'Edit' link are red, while Billing/Settings/Account buttons, badges, toggles, and sliders are purple/indigo.
problems_to_fix.fix: Unify to ONE brand accent token `--accent` used everywhere (primary actions, active states, selection, focus rings). Remove the separate purple/indigo palette entirely from these surfaces. Update tailwind.config.js and globals.css so there is exactly one accent family, not a `red` palette plus a separate `accent: {coral, terracotta, gold}` plus indigo/purple utility classes scattered in pages.

problems_to_fix.problem: Misleading progress bar. Account → Subscription shows 'Generations 0 (Unlimited)' as a full RED bar — reads like an error or maxed quota.
problems_to_fix.fix: For unlimited plans (generations_limit <= 0): show NO filled bar — replace with an 'Unlimited' chip / ∞ symbol. Filled bars are only for real consumed/total ratios, and must never render danger-red for a normal/healthy state (use --accent or a neutral/positive tone, reserve red for genuine warning/error states ≥ ~85% usage).

problems_to_fix.problem: Data & copy bugs: pricing rows duplicate numbers ('1 1 studios included', '2 2 studios included', '3 3 studios included') and use awkward copy ('none support').
problems_to_fix.fix: Fix the duplicate-number rendering in the Billing page so each row reads '1 studio included' / '2 studios included' / '3 studios included' (singular/plural handled correctly, number shown once). Map raw support_level values to proper display copy on the frontend ONLY — do not change backend values: none → 'No support', email → 'Email support', priority → 'Priority support', dedicated → 'Dedicated support'.

problems_to_fix.problem: Inconsistent plan labels across pages ('Free' vs 'Premium (Enterprise)').
problems_to_fix.fix: Establish a single source of truth for displaying the user's current plan name (one shared formatting/lookup used by Account, Billing, and Settings) so the same plan renders identically everywhere. Frontend display-layer fix only — do not change how the backend stores plan/tier data.

problems_to_fix.problem: Inconsistent styling of form fields, button radius, and section spacing between Billing, Settings, and Account.
problems_to_fix.fix: Unify via design tokens: one radius scale, one spacing rhythm (8px base, tiers 16/24/32/48), one set of input/button styles applied consistently across all three pages.

problems_to_fix.problem: Numbered list used as a stepper with no progress feedback.
problems_to_fix.fix: Replace with a real animated stepper: active dot fills with --accent plus a single gentle pulse on arrival, completed steps animate a checkmark, connector line fills as the user advances. Visual/motion change only — do not alter the underlying step logic or data.

problems_to_fix.problem: Weak mobile adaptation on every page and the dashboard.
problems_to_fix.fix: Apply the full responsive system described below to landing, billing, settings, account, and the dashboard shell — mobile-first, breakpoints 375 / 768 / 1024 / 1440, no horizontal scroll, ≥44px touch targets.
Restyle into calm bento-style cards with breathing room (varied spacing tiers, not one uniform gap) — visual restyle only, the rail's content/data sources stay exactly as they are.

design_vision.summary: Warm, minimalist, motion-light interface — a 'soft bento' layout. The feeling: a premium, modern AI studio — calm, confident, effortless. NOT a corporate SaaS table, NOT a neon hype gradient.
moodboard_cues: Soft bento layout: content in large rounded cards on a grid with generous gaps; calm and scannable.
Warm, airy palette: a subtle cream → soft-lilac ambient background gradient behind crisp white cards, with a few pastel accent tiles (lemon, mint, peach, lilac) used sparingly — max 1–2 per view.
Big, confident typography: oversized, tight display headings/numerals (think a giant clock readout) paired with quiet, readable body text.
Floating pill controls: rounded nav, segmented toggles, soft switches that feel tactile.
Restrained motion: gentle fades, springy scale on press, staggered card entrances, smooth shared-element transitions between steps. Nothing flashy; nothing that blocks input.

look_human_designed_not_ai_generated.note: Read carefully — the result must look like a senior designer crafted it with intent, not a default AI/template output. Treat as hard requirements.
avoid_these_ai_tells: Default purple/indigo → blue gradients, gradient-filled headings, glowing 'AI' gradients
Everything centered, everything the same size; three identical feature cards in a perfect row with generic round icons
Emoji used as icons or section bullets; generic sparkle/rocket/checkmark emoji decoration
Over-rounded blobs everywhere, heavy uniform drop shadows, glassmorphism on every surface
Placeholder/lorem text or vague filler copy ('Empower your workflow'); repeating the same icon for different things
Symmetric, evenly-spaced 'safe' layouts with no focal point; uniform 16px gaps everywhere with no rhythm
A wall of equal-weight text with no clear hierarchy; tiny gray-on-gray captions
do_these_instead: Opinionated hierarchy — one clear focal point per screen; deliberate size jumps (a genuinely large display heading vs. quiet body)
Intentional asymmetry & editorial layout — bento tiles of varied sizes, off-grid accents, content that breathes
Real, specific copy — concrete labels and microcopy, real plan names and numbers, never lorem
Consistent professional icon system — lucide only, uniform 1.5px stroke, outline style throughout, restrained tinted containers (no rainbow circles, no mixing filled/outline at the same level)
One or two memorable signature details (e.g. studio-card selected ring + glow, before/after slider handle, stepper connector that fills, pill toggle thumb) — not ten
Restraint with color — mostly neutral surfaces + warm ambient gradient; accent appears only where it means something (primary action, active state, selection); pastel tiles max 1–2 per view
Deliberate spacing rhythm — 8px scale with varied tiers (16/24/32/48) to group and separate, not one uniform gap
Typographic craft — tight tracking on big display text, comfortable line-height on body, tabular numerals for data, real font pairing
Considered empty/loading/error states — make these thoughtful, not lazy filler
look_human_designed_not_ai_generated.litmus_test: If a screen could be any generic SaaS starter template, it's wrong. It should feel specific to AutoStudio — an automotive AI photo studio — calm, premium, and a little editorial.

motion_spec.global_rules: Every animation conveys cause→effect. Animate only transform/opacity. Max 1–2 animated elements per view. Exits faster than entrances (~65%). Always honor prefers-reduced-motion (drop to instant/opacity-only). Never block input. Animations interruptible by tap/gesture.
patterns: Page/step transitions: shared-element + directional. Forward = enter from below/right (fade + translateY 8px); back = reverse. Active step content cross-fades; stepper indicator slides.
Card entrances: staggered 30–50ms/item, translateY 12px→0 + fade, ease-out, base duration.
Option/studio/pricing cards: hover lift (translateY −2px + shadow-md); press scale 0.98 spring; on select → animated check badge (scale 0.6→1 spring) + accent ring draws in + soft accent-soft glow.
Buttons: press scale 0.97 spring; primary CTA subtle hover sheen (opacity-driven). Loading = swap label for inline spinner, keep width stable, disable.
Stepper: active dot fills accent + a single gentle pulse on arrival; completed steps animate a checkmark; connector line fills as you advance.
Upload dropzone: drag-over → animated dashed border + accent-soft tint + 4px icon bob. On drop → thumbnail scales in (0.9→1).
Processing: calm premium loading — shimmering skeleton of the result frame + soft indeterminate bar OR % counter (tabular numerals). NO hype spinner. Rotating reassurance copy.
Result reveal: before/after draggable comparison slider; AI result fades/scales in (0.98→1) when ready; Download button shows a one-time success checkmark flash after click.
Toasts: slide + fade from edge, auto-dismiss 3–5s, aria-live='polite', never steal focus.
Number changes (credits/usage): quick count-up with tabular figures so layout never jumps.
Theme toggle: crossfade surfaces; do not flash.
Segmented toggles (Monthly/Yearly): animated thumb slide between options.

layout_system.desktop_1024_plus: Sticky left workflow sidebar · centered content column (cards max-width ~880px) · right rail (usage + recent + upsell). Ambient gradient behind everything; cards float with shadow-md.
layout_system.dashboard_bento: Mixed card sizes (hero 'Start new project' + usage + recent projects + tips) on a responsive grid — visual restyle of existing cards only, do not change what data each card shows.
layout_system.container: Consistent max width; adaptive gutters: 16px mobile → 24px tablet → 32–48px desktop.
layout_system.z_index_scale: 0 base · 10 sticky bars · 20 dropdowns · 40 drawers · 100 modals · 1000 toasts.

mobile_responsive_required_for_all_pages.principles: Mobile-first. Breakpoints 375 / 768 / 1024 / 1440. No horizontal scroll. Use min-h-dvh not 100vh. Respect safe areas (notch, Dynamic Island, home indicator). Test at 375px and landscape.
requirements: Top nav → mobile: logo + avatar + hamburger; primary destinations in a bottom tab bar (max 5, icon + label, active highlighted, safe-area padding) OR a slide-in drawer.
Workflow stepper → mobile: compact 'Step X of 5' + segmented progress; sticky bottom Continue/Back bar (height ≥44px).
Studio / pricing grids → mobile: 2-up (or 1-up smallest); comfortable spacing.
Right rail → mobile: reflows below content as stacked cards (Usage first); Go Pro as a single card; nothing critical hidden.
Dashboard bento → mobile: single column, core content first, secondary content folded below.
Billing/Account tables & rows → mobile: stacked cards, never horizontal scroll.
Touch: targets ≥44×44px, ≥8px apart, touch-action: manipulation, feedback within 100ms, no hover-only actions.
Forms → mobile: input height ≥44px, semantic keyboards (email/tel/number), autofill attributes, password show/hide.

design_tokens_to_consolidate.note: Currently the codebase has a partial/conflicting token system (tailwind.config.js defines a separate `red` palette AND a separate `accent: {coral, terracotta, gold}` palette; globals.css defines both --accent-red and --accent-coral). Consolidate this into ONE coherent token system — this is itself part of fixing problem #2.
required_token_groups: Single --accent (+ --accent-soft for tints/glows) — replacing both the red family and the coral/terracotta/gold family on these surfaces
Warm ambient background tokens (cream → soft-lilac gradient stops)
Neutral surface/text scale (keep consistent with existing charcoal/warm tones where they already work)
Radius scale, spacing scale (8/16/24/32/48), shadow scale (card / card-lg / card-hover)
Motion tokens (durations + easing curves) for the patterns listed above

verification_before_done: Screenshot or recording of the landing page at desktop width AND at 375px mobile width
Screenshot or recording of Billing, Settings, and Account pages at desktop AND 375px mobile width, showing the unified accent color and consistent styling
Screenshot of the Account 'Unlimited' usage state showing the chip/∞ instead of a full red bar
Screenshot of the Billing pricing rows showing corrected copy ('1 studio included', 'No support', etc., no duplicated numbers)
Screenshot of the dashboard shell restyled (NOT restructured) at desktop and mobile widths

`git diff --stat` proving: (a) no files under backend/** were touched, (b) no changes to dashboard component logic/data-fetching files — only style/markup-level changes in pages, globals.css, and tailwind.config.js
Confirmation that any new/changed copy exists in both en.json and sv.json
final_step_instructions_for_claude_code: After all visual changes are made and verified locally (screenshots + tsc + diff as listed above), STOP.
Do NOT run `git push`. Do NOT open a pull request. Do NOT push to GitHub.
Report back with: a summary of what was changed, the verification evidence listed above, and the exact `git status` / `git diff --stat` output.
Then explicitly WAIT for the user's permission before pushing anything to GitHub. The user will review the local result first and give a clear go-ahead before any push happens.
communication_rules: If you discover that fixing the accent-color clash properly requires editing tailwind.config.js / globals.css in a way that affects pages outside this list, say so explicitly before doing it — don't silently expand scope.
If something the brief asks for would require touching backend code or restructuring the dashboard, stop and flag it — do not work around the constraint by changing scope quietly.
Do not claim a step is done without pasting the actual evidence (screenshot, command output) in the same message.
